The '''''Pulsar Skate''''' was a modified [[Baudo-class star yacht|''Baudo''-class star yacht]] owned by [[Booster Terrik]] and later inherited by his daughter [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax]].

==History==
Terrik presumably acquired the ''Pulsar Skate'' at some point after [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], when he was using a stolen freighter named the ''[[Starwayman]]''. He named his new ship after a [[Pulsar skate/Legends|sea creature it resembled]], and used it in his [[Spice/Legends|spice]]-[[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] operations.

One recurring port of call for the ''Pulsar Skate'' in these early days was the [[Gus Treta Inner-System Market Station|Gus Treta space station]] in the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]], where Terrik's friends [[Jagged Antilles|Jagged]] and [[Zena Antilles]] operated the refueling depot. In [[6 BBY/Legends|6 BBY]], their fifteen-year-old son [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge]] replaced the ship's starboard fusion engine.

The next year, the ''Pulsar Skate'' was once again at Gus Treta, when [[Loka Hask]] and his [[Bonestar Pirates]] destroyed the refuelling depot, killing Wedge's parents. Booster maneuvered the ship clear, while his co-[[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], [[Polipe]], kept the boy restrained on the flight deck.

After Booster was arrested, his daughter [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax]] took over that part of his operations that were not seized by [[Jorj Car'das]], and continued to use the ''Pulsar Skate'' in the same way. In [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], the ship came under attack by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] [[Corusca Rainbow|''Black Asp'']], but was rescued by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. During this battle, Corran Horn was rescued by the ship, because his X-Wing had been damaged and was unable to fly properly. Mirax and her pilot, [[Liat Tsayv]], agreed to use the ''Skate'' in smuggling operations for the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and it was vital in the effort to supply [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] when it was ravaged by the [[Krytos virus]]. The ''Pulsar Skate'', using the alias ''[[Merisee Hope]]'', was also vital in transporting Rogue Squadron pilots to the surface of Coruscant in an effort to take the planetary shields down. After leaving the ship in the hands of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], Mirax found Wedge and helped in the shield operation.

[[File:PulsarSkate-CCG.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Pulsar Skate'' in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].]]

Mirax continued to use the ''Pulsar Skate'' after her marriage to Rogue Squadron pilot [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], establishing herself primarily as an interstellar art dealer. However, she continued to help out her husband and his friends, and in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the ship took [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Anakin Solo]], and [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] to [[Vortex/Legends|Vortex]] to search for [[Daeshara'cor]].

In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the ''Pulsar Skate'' was involved in the evacuation the [[Horn family]] and [[Antilles family/Legends|Antilles family]] from [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] when premier [[Dur Gejjen]] turned against Wedge during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]].

==Behind the scenes==
There is something of a discrepancy about the appearance of the ''Pulsar Skate''. The visual depictions of the ship in the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron]]'' comics and [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''CCG'']] have been internally consistent, but these do not match the earlier illustration of the [[Baudo-class star yacht|''Baudo''-class]] design in ''[[Galaxy Guide 6: Tramp Freighters]]'' and ''[[Pirates & Privateers]]'', which seems to have served as the basis for some descriptions in novels, most recently in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]''. No [[canon]]ical explanation has been offered to resolve the discrepancy.
